title: Osmangazi

infobox.value: is a municipality and [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Turkey "Districts of Turkey") of [Bursa Province](/wiki/Bursa_Province "Bursa Province"), [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Its area is 621 km2,[[3]](#cite_note-3) and its population is 891,250 (2022).[[1]](#cite_note-tuik-1) It is one of the central metropolitan districts of the city of [Bursa](/wiki/Bursa "Bursa"), as well as the fourth largest overall municipality in [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"). On its own, it would be the 28th largest city in Turkey.

outgoing_links.target_title: History of the Republic of Turkey

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/Bursa_7017.jpg/250px-Bursa_7017.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bursa_7017.jpg)

Fountain of brotherhood between Osmangazi, Bursa and [Stari Grad, Sarajevo](/wiki/Stari_Grad,_Sarajevo "Stari Grad, Sarajevo") in Bursa.

**Osmangazi** is a municipality and [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Turkey "Districts of Turkey") of [Bursa Province](/wiki/Bursa_Province "Bursa Province"), [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Its area is 621 km2,[[3]](#cite_note-3) and its population is 891,250 (2022).[[1]](#cite_note-tuik-1) It is one of the central metropolitan districts of the city of [Bursa](/wiki/Bursa "Bursa"), as well as the fourth largest overall municipality in [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"). On its own, it would be the 28th largest city in Turkey.

The district has been shaped by several civilizations: [Rome](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire"), [Byzantines](/wiki/Byzantine_Empire "Byzantine Empire"), [Seljuqians](/wiki/Seljuq_dynasty "Seljuq dynasty"), and [Ottomans](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire"). The first marks of the Ottoman Empire exist within Osmangazi where it extended from the foot of [Uludağ](/wiki/Uluda%C4%9F "Uludağ") to the plain of Bursa. Within the boundaries of Osmangazi, approximately 1800 registered historical buildings exist.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Osmangazi&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

It was stated that there were various settlements in Bursa and its surroundings by 4000 B.C. But the certain information related to the region belongs to 700 B.C. [Homer](/wiki/Homer "Homer") called the region [Mysia](/wiki/Mysia "Mysia"). There are two villages that are ancient Mysia settlements: Misi (Gümüştepe) and Misebolu. The region is called [Phrygia](/wiki/Phrygia "Phrygia") in historical geography. It is known that [Cimmerians](/wiki/Cimmerians "Cimmerians"), fleeing from the migration of the [Scythians](/wiki/Scythians "Scythians"), put an end to Phrygian sovereignty. The name of Bursa comes from the founder of this city, Prussias, the King of [Bithynia](/wiki/Bithynia "Bithynia"). The Bithynians, who migrated to the region in 7th century B.C., used the name Bithynia.

It was pointed out that one of the most important commanders that [Carthage](/wiki/Carthage "Carthage") had recommended the establishment of the City of Prusias and Olympus to King Prusian I. in 185 B.C. By that time, "Prusa" has been transformed to Bursa.

When Bithynia joined the [Roman Empire](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire") in 74 B.C., it became the [Asian Province](/wiki/Asia_(Roman_province) "Asia (Roman province)") that was governed by [proconsuls](/wiki/Proconsuls "Proconsuls") (head of the province), who were appointed in [Rome](/wiki/Ancient_Rome "Ancient Rome").

Bursa was under the sovereignty of the [Byzantine Empire](/wiki/Byzantine_Empire "Byzantine Empire") between 385 and 1326 A.D. The city became a thermal city after the [thermal baths](/wiki/Thermal_baths "Thermal baths") while [silk](/wiki/Silk "Silk") production in the region started in 550 A.D. Bursa was seized by [Osman I](/wiki/Osman_I "Osman I") in 1307 and conquered by [Orhan](/wiki/Orhan "Orhan"), the son of Osman I, on 6 April 1335. The capital of the empire was moved to Bursa in 1335 and new development movements were initiated. When Bursa was conquered, the city consisted of the inner castle, but Orhan had the Orhan Gazi Complex built out of the town. Public buildings such as mosques, baths, soup houses, hospitals, [madrasahs](/wiki/Madrasahs "Madrasahs") were built out of the walls, where new settlements had been created, hence a new tradition of settling area had been established. Right after the conquest of Istanbul by Sultan [Mehmed II](/wiki/Mehmed_II "Mehmed II")., the active role of Bursa and its qualification as the administrative center had faded away. There are no buildings surviving from the Roman and Byzantine era. It is believed that the walls surrounding the city were built by Bithynians and used by the Romans, Byzantines, and Ottomans after maintenance.

Bursa had developed more than other cities in the first two centuries of Ottoman rule. It was filled with a number of architectural structures and became a trade city as well as a science city, where various famous madrasahs were located. Hüdavendigar Complex of Sultan Murat I., Yıldırım Complex of Sultan Bayezid I., and Green Complex all began their construction under Sultan Mehmed I. and were finished by Sultan [Murad II](/wiki/Murad_II "Murad II"). They affected the spatial development of Bursa and still exist to this day. Industrialization within the city was accelerated in the [republican era](/wiki/History_of_the_Republic_of_Turkey "History of the Republic of Turkey"), so that the population and the urban development of the city changed in the 1960s. The geographical location and the agricultural and industrial potential have increased the attraction of the city.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Osmangazi&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Osmangazi, the center of social and economic relations for years, is in the crossroad of roads to İzmir, İstanbul and Eskişehir. Osmangazi is the biggest district of Bursa that is on the southwest coast of [Marmara Sea](/wiki/Marmara_Sea "Marmara Sea"). Gökdere River on the east, Nilüfer River and Mudanya Road on the west, Katırlı Mountains on the north, Uludağ and Kirazlı Plateau are the natural and artificial boundaries of the district.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Osmangazi&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Economy")]

The following types of industries exist in Osmangazi: [automotive](/wiki/Automotive "Automotive") supply industry, [textile](/wiki/Textile "Textile"), [towel](/wiki/Towel "Towel"), knitter & tricot, shoe industry, [agricultural machines](/wiki/Agricultural_machine "Agricultural machine"), [furniture](/wiki/Furniture "Furniture"), [leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather"), plastic, machine and [metal industry](/wiki/Metal_industry "Metal industry"), electric, foundry, [welding machine](/wiki/Welding_machine "Welding machine"), stove and knife sectors. Moreover, there are also oil and flour factories.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Osmangazi&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Education")]

Two vocational high schools are located in the district, [Bursa Sports High School](/wiki/Bursa_Sports_High_School "Bursa Sports High School") at Sevgi St. in Veysel Karani neighborhood,[[5]](#cite_note-bb1-5) and [Bursa Agriculture Vocational High School](/wiki/Bursa_Agriculture_Vocational_High_School "Bursa Agriculture Vocational High School") at Hürriyer St. in YeniKaraman neighborhood.[[6]](#cite_note-tkp1-6)

## Main sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Osmangazi&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Main sights")]

* Reşat Oyal Culture Park, which is the symbol of Bursa,
* İnkaya Plane which is the natural monument of 500 years old,
* Tophane Slopes,
* Kozahan, which is located between Ulu Mosque and Orhan Mosque,
* Emirhan which was built by Orhan Bey in 1340, Muradiye Complex which is composed of madrasah, school, soup house, bath and mosque, and more than twenty tombs including [Murat II](/wiki/Murat_II "Murat II"). and [Cem Sultan](/wiki/Cem_Sultan "Cem Sultan"),
* [Hüdavendigar Mosque](/wiki/H%C3%BCdavendigar_Mosque "Hüdavendigar Mosque"),
* [Grand Mosque of Bursa](/wiki/Grand_Mosque_of_Bursa "Grand Mosque of Bursa"), which is the sample of multi-pillared and multi-domed mosques,
* Merinos City Park,
* Botanical Park,
* Soğanlı Zoo,
* Pınarbaşı Park,
* Soğukkuyu Park,
* Hamitler Park,
* Cable Water Ski Center.

## Historical monuments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Osmangazi&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Historical monuments")]

* Bursa Castle
* Bursa Fortress,
* Tomb of Murad I.,
* Tomb of Pars Bey,
* Çakır Ağa Bath,
* Tombs of Osmangazi and Orhangazi,
* Tomb of Şehzade Mustafa, Bazaar,
* [Archeology Museum](/wiki/Bursa_Archaeological_Museum "Bursa Archaeological Museum"),
* [Atatürk Museum](/wiki/Bursa_Atat%C3%BCrk_Museum "Bursa Atatürk Museum"),
* [Museum of Ottoman House](/wiki/Museum_of_Ottoman_House "Museum of Ottoman House"),
* City Museum,
* Ördekli Culture Center,
